goal: An electronics export company based in Thailand is expanding e-commerce operations into Singapore and Indonesia. They collect customer personal data (names, addresses, payment info) and need to understand: (1) what cross-border data transfer rules apply when sending customer data from SG/ID to their Thai HQ servers, (2) what data localization requirements exist in each market, and (3) what data protection obligations they must comply with as a foreign data controller. Extract and map all relevant clauses from Singapore PDPA and Indonesia PDP Law to RDTII Pillar 6 and Pillar 7.
why: Simulate real usage by a digital trader / export-import company assessing regulatory compliance before market entry
constraints: flag any clause with confidence < 0.7 for human review
include cross-border transfer rules, localization requirements, and foreign controller obligations
deliverable: evidence records for SG PDPA cross-border transfer rules (Pillar 6)
evidence records for SG PDPA data protection obligations (Pillar 7)
evidence records for ID PDP Law cross-border transfer rules (Pillar 6)
evidence records for ID PDP Law data protection obligations (Pillar 7)
compliance gap summary for Thai exporter
doneCriteria: at least 2 clauses extracted per jurisdiction per pillar
